source_pr.title: oomtest: add filter to avoid flaky test in the oomCapture
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: Weizhen Wang <wangweizhen@pingcap.com>

<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #39434, #39226, [#39225](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/39225)

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory

Documentation

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note

```

symptoms: OOM capture tests intermittently record an unrelated background log message as the "tracker" message, causing assertions to fail.
failure_signature: `oomCapture.tracker` is overwritten by background `BgLogger().Info("SetTiFlashGroupConfig")`, leading to wrong captured message / flaky assertions.
root_cause_explanation: The OOM test installs `oomCapture` as a zap core hook and records the last seen `entry.Message` in `h.tracker`. A common background task logs `SetTiFlashGroupConfig`, which can arrive concurrently and overwrite `h.tracker` with an irrelevant message, making the test flaky. The fix explicitly ignores that background log message in `oomCapture.Write` so only OOM-relevant messages update the tracker.
fix_pattern: Filter out known unrelated background log messages in the OOM log-capture hook (`oomCapture.Write`) so the captured tracker message is deterministic.

evidence: evidence: oomtest: add filter to avoid flaky test in the oomCapture
